weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Still hypertrophy: moderate loads, higher reps, controlled tempo. Every pattern is fresh this week — read the cues, settle into a load that lets you finish each set clean, and cap it at 3 sets. Progress by adding a rep or slowing the tempo, never a fourth set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 25 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · steady comp rhythm, full sit and full stand
patterns.load: Your competition KB at competition box height
cues: Settle into a steady pace you can hold for all 25 reps without stopping.
Touch the box softly each rep and reverse without rocking backward.
Lock your hips fully at the top so every rep counts as a full rep.
Keep the bell still at your chest — do not let it pull you forward.

patterns.name: Goblet Squat 1.5-Rep
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · down, half up, down, then all the way up = 1 rep
patterns.load: A KB about 1 size lighter than your competition KB, held at the chest
cues: Squat to the bottom, rise only halfway, sink back down, then stand fully.
Stay tall through the extra half-rep so your chest never drops.
Move smoothly through the bottom — do not bounce out of the hole.
Count one full sequence as a single rep so you do not shortchange the set.

patterns.name: Split-Stance KB RDL
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each leg · rest 75 sec between sets · hinge back, feel the back of the thigh
patterns.load: One KB held in front, light enough to keep a flat back all 12 reps
cues: Push your hips straight back and let the KB slide down your front leg.
Keep a soft bend in the front knee and your back flat the whole time.
Feel the stretch in the back of the working thigh, then stand by squeezing your glute.
Keep most of your weight on the front foot — the back foot is for balance only.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 throws · rest 75 sec between sets · 15 controlled throws total
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Sink into a low hinge, then snap up and throw the ball up and back over your head for height and distance.
Drive the throw with your hips snapping open — let your arms simply guide the ball overhead.
Reset your feet and breathe before each throw so every rep is crisp.
Keep your stomach braced so your low back doesn't arch as the ball leaves your hands.

patterns.name: Good Morning — Bodyweight to Light Bar
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight with hands across your chest, or a light bar / 10–15 lb on your upper back
cues: With a soft knee bend, push your hips back and let your chest fold toward the floor until you feel your hamstrings stretch, then stand tall.
Keep your back flat and your shins vertical — the motion is hips going back, not knees bending.
Only fold as far as you can with a flat back; stop short if your back wants to round.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top to finish each rep.

patterns.name: Half-Kneeling Lat Pulldown — Band
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A resistance band anchored overhead, tension set so rep 15 is still controlled
cues: Kneel tall on one knee and pull the band down to your chest, driving your elbows toward your ribs.
Feel your lats and the muscles under your arms working — this builds the pull that controls the ball overhead.
Keep your torso upright and your stomach braced; don't lean back to yank the band down.
Return slowly to full overhead reach each rep so you train the whole range.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~68% of your max-rep test · rest 75 sec between · 1-sec pause on the chest
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — hit it on all 3 sets with a 1-second pause on the chest each rep.
Keep the rest tight at 75 seconds so you accumulate quality reps without fully recovering between sets.
Stop a set the moment the bar stalls or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Flat DB Flye
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two light DBs you can control for 15 reps with a slight elbow bend
cues: Lie flat, hold the DBs over your chest with a soft bend in the elbows, and open your arms wide in a slow arc.
Feel the stretch across your chest at the bottom, then hug the DBs back together over your chest.
Keep the elbows softly bent the whole time and go light — this is a stretch and squeeze, not a press.

patterns.name: Close-Grip DB Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: Two moderate DBs held together you can press for 12 reps
cues: Press two DBs straight up with your hands close and your elbows tucked tight to your sides.
The narrow path hits your triceps and inner chest — keep the DBs over your lower chest.
Lower under control to a light touch on your chest and press back up. Stop if the elbows start flaring out.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — hold to 80% and step down, keep it sub-max
cues: Stay packed at the shoulders the entire hold; ribs down, body quiet under the bar.
Spread your fingers wide and grip the bar as if trying to crush it.
Exhale slowly and steadily; do not clench and hold your breath.
Step down on a controlled note and write down the time you reached.

patterns.name: Fat-Grip Farmer Carry
patterns.rx: 3 × 40-yard walk · rest 90 sec between carries
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand heavy enough that your grip is fading by the end of 40 yards · fat grips on the handles
cues: Stand tall with shoulders pulled back and walk smooth, even steps — no leaning.
Let the fat grips overload your open hand; crush them the whole way.
If a dumbbell starts to slip, set it down safely and that is your distance.
Keep your core braced so the weight does not pull you side to side.

patterns.name: Lat Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 14 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Cable weight you can pull for a controlled 14 with about 2 reps left at the end
cues: Pull the bar to your collarbone by driving your elbows down, not by leaning way back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades down and together at the bottom of each rep.
Let the bar rise slowly until your arms are fully straight and your lats stretch.
Keep your chest up the whole set so the lats do the work, not momentum.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 18 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Full overhead reach on every rep, then slam straight down between your feet.
Keep your reps even across all three sets — pace it so set three looks like set one.
Breathe out on the slam and stay relaxed in the shoulders between reps.

patterns.name: Seated D-Ball Overhead Slam
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 8 lb (women) / 15 lb (men)
cues: Sit tall on a box, reach the ball overhead, then slam it down just in front of your feet.
All the work comes from your arms, lats, and trunk — your legs can't cheat here.
Sit upright the whole set; don't round your back to reach the floor.

patterns.name: Straight-Arm Cable Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A moderate cable weight you can control for 15 reps
cues: With arms nearly straight, pull the bar from overhead down to your thighs, then return under control.
Feel your lats do the work; keep a soft bend in the elbows but don't turn it into a triceps press.
Drive the bar down on the same overhead-to-thigh path your slam uses, leading with your lats on every rep.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 90 sec between sets · smooth, even rhythm
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at an easy-to-hold comp pace
cues: Find a rhythm you could talk through and hold it the whole minute.
Land softly on the balls of both feet — quiet feet means good rhythm.
If you trip, restart immediately and keep the clock running rather than stopping.

patterns.name: Toes-Elevated Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 22 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · 2-sec hold at the top
patterns.load: A DB or KB in each hand heavy enough that the last 2 of 22 reps are tough with a 2-sec top hold · toes on a 2-inch plate or board
cues: Let your heels drop below the plate at the bottom for a deep stretch.
Drive up tall onto your toes and pause at the very top.
Keep your knees straight so the calf does the work, not your thighs.

patterns.name: In-Place Pogo Bounce
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 sec continuous bounces · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, light springy bounces
cues: Bounce off the balls of your feet with stiff, springy ankles — keep your knees nearly straight.
Stay tall and light, like a pogo stick, instead of squatting between bounces.
Land in the same spot each time and keep the bounces small and quick.

patterns.name: DB Romanian Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · 3-sec lower on every rep
patterns.load: A DB in each hand, heavy enough that the last 2 of 15 reps are hard with a flat back
cues: Push your hips straight back and let the DBs travel close to your legs — knees soft but mostly still.
Stand tall by squeezing your glutes; power comes from the hips, not from rounding the lower back.
Lower slowly and feel the stretch in your hamstrings. Stop the set the moment your back wants to round.

patterns.name: Wall-Supported Wall Sit
patterns.rx: 3 × 40 sec holds · rest 60 sec between holds
patterns.load: Bodyweight, back flat against a wall with thighs near parallel to the floor
cues: Slide down the wall until your knees are bent about 90 degrees and hold there, weight in your heels.
Keep your back flat against the wall and your knees tracking over your toes — don't let them cave in.
Breathe steadily through the hold. Stand up early if your knees ache; come down only as far as is comfortable.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · steady, repeatable reach
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing the arms back, dip the hips, and jump forward landing softly on both feet at the same time.
Aim for the same comfortable distance on every rep so each landing stays controlled and quiet.
Walk back to the line and fully reset between jumps. Keep them clean, not maximal, this week.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 500m · rest 3 min fully between · hold one steady split across all three
patterns.load: Rower · a pace about 4–6 sec/500m slower than all-out, the same split on every piece
cues: Pick one split number and hold it — piece one should look exactly like piece three.
Push the footplates away with straight arms first, then open the back, then draw the handle in.
Long, smooth recovery up the slide — slower coming forward than you went back.

patterns.name: Single-Arm DB Bench Pull-Through
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps each arm · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: One DB heavy enough that the last 2 reps each arm are hard at 12
cues: Brace one hand and knee on a bench, let the DB hang, then pull it to your hip and lower it all the way down.
Lead with the elbow and keep it close to your side — don't let the arm flare out wide.
Keep your back flat like a tabletop; don't twist your spine to lift the weight.

patterns.name: Banded Straight-Arm Pulldown
patterns.rx: 3 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A resistance band with enough tension that the last few reps burn at 15
cues: Anchor a band overhead, keep your arms straight, and pull the band down to your thighs.
Squeeze the lats at the bottom and let the band rise slowly back up under control.
Keep a soft bend in the elbows but don't bend them to cheat — the lats do the work.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 250-yd shuttle (5 × 50 yd) · rest 3–4 min between · controlled turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · hold a steady comp-rehearsal pace across all five lengths
cues: Run five 50-yard lengths at a strong but controlled pace you could sustain across the full 300.
Ease off a step before each turn, plant, and push back off — smooth turns beat hard cuts when training alone.
Take the full rest between sets so every set holds the same honest pace.

patterns.name: Step-Up Repeats
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 reps (10 per leg) · controlled · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight on a knee-height box, or hold 10–20 lb dumbbells if that feels easy
cues: Step up driving through the whole foot until your hip and knee are fully straight at the top.
Lower yourself down under control instead of dropping — your working leg does the work, not a push off the floor.
Accumulate all 20 quality reps per set for the legs and lungs.

patterns.name: Lateral Shuffle Lane
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 yards (15 down, 15 back) · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Stay in a low athletic stance and shuffle sideways without clicking your heels together.
Keep your chest up and hips low — this builds the side-to-side control that protects your shuttle turns.
Moderate, repeatable effort across all three sets, not a max sprint.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 90 sec between · steady, controlled (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Push the full 50 yards at a controlled pace and rack up quality steps — this is sled volume, not a timed test.
Body at about 45°, arms locked, pushing through your trunk — don't let your elbows bend to soak up the load.
Long steps, full hip extension, and drive through your toes on every stride.

patterns.name: DB Step-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps per leg · controlled · rest 75 sec between
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand onto a knee-height box or bench, heavy enough that the final reps per leg are challenging
cues: Plant your whole foot on the box and stand up by driving through that heel — push the leg up, don't push off the floor with your trailing foot.
Lower yourself back down slowly under control instead of dropping, so the working leg does the work the whole rep.
Keep the box at a height where your knee stays comfortable — lower the box if your knee or hip complains.

patterns.name: Chest-Supported DB Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · controlled tempo · rest 75 sec between
patterns.load: A dumbbell in each hand, lying chest-down on an incline bench, heavy enough that the last 2 reps are hard but clean
cues: Lie face-down on the incline so your chest is supported, then row both dumbbells up by driving your elbows toward your hips.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top to build the upper-back strength that braces your sled push.
Lower the weights all the way down under control — don't yank or let momentum swing them up.
